title: Inject an HTTP Fault with VirtualService
description:
## Inject an HTTP Fault with VirtualService

Fault injection is a chaos-engineering technique to test how services handle failures. Istio can inject HTTP errors and delays without modifying application code.

**Your task:**

A `VirtualService` named `ratings` already exists in the `istio-lab` namespace. Update it to inject an **HTTP 500 error** for **100%** of requests to the `ratings` service.

The fault must be defined under `spec.http[0].fault.abort` with:
- `httpStatus: 500`
- `percentage.value: 100`

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get virtualservice ratings -n istio-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.http[0].fault.abort.httpStatus}'
```

hints.title: VirtualService fault injection structure
hints.body: Add a fault.abort block inside the http[] rule. The percentage.value field controls what fraction of requests are aborted.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: ratings
  namespace: istio-lab
spec:
  hosts:
  - ratings
  http:
  - fault:
      abort:
        httpStatus: 500
        percentage:
          value: 100
    route:
    - destination:
        host: ratings
EOF

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: ratings
  namespace: istio-lab
spec:
  hosts:
  - ratings
  http:
  - route:
    - destination:
        host: ratings
EOF

validation.description: Check that the VirtualService has an HTTP 500 abort fault at 100%
